name: ElmChat

aliases.name: ExtraAttributes
aliases.comment:
Extra attributes for the UI components.

This is the initial value of `Settings.attributes`.

You'll usually create one by changing `defaultAttributes`.
aliases.type: { chatTable : List (Attribute msg), sizeButtons : List (Attribute msg), sizeColumn : List (Attribute msg), textColumn : List (Attribute msg), textArea : List (Attribute msg) }

aliases.name: Sender
aliases.comment: A function to turn an input string and a `Settings` record into a `Msg`.

aliases.name: Settings
aliases.comment:
Settings for the chat component.

Make one of these with `makeSettings`, and store it in your model.
aliases.type: { fontSize : Int, text : String, input : String, scroll : Float, attributes : ExtraAttributes msg, id : String, defaultFontSize : Int, showSizeControls : Bool, updater : TheUpdater msg }

aliases.name: Updater
aliases.comment: A function to turn a `Settings` record and a `Cmd` into a `Msg`.

values.name: addChat
values.comment: Add a line to the chat box.
values.type: Settings msg -> String -> ( Settings msg, Cmd msg )

values.name: defaultExtraAttributes
values.comment: The default value of the `Settings.attributes` property.

values.name: inputBox
values.comment:
Create a text input control.

Args are `textSize buttonText sender settings`.

`textSize` is the width in characters of the input control.

`buttonText` is the text for the button that sends the input.

`sender` is a function to turn an input string and `settings into a `Msg`.

`settings` is your `Settings` record.
values.type: Int -> String -> Sender msg -> Settings msg -> Html msg

values.name: makeSettings
values.comment:
Make a Settings record to add to your Model.

Args are `id initialFontSize showSizeControls updater`.

`id` is the Html id for the textarea showing the chat.

`initialFontSize` is the initial font size of the textarea in `pt`.

`showSizeControls` is `True` to show the font size controls to the left of the text area.

`updater` will be called to generate messages to update the Settings in your Model.
values.type: String -> Int -> Bool -> Updater msg -> Settings msg
